author: Ameen Hassan
summary: Session focused on registering the ameen-context-mcp server globally in Claude Code. Two approaches failed: adding mcpServers to settings.json (rejected by schema validation) and creating ~/.claude/.mcp.json (not picked up by Claude Code). The repo was confirmed pushed to GitHub at ahassan507/ameen-context-mcp with 11 files. The session ended mid-execution while attempting the correct approach: `claude mcp add ameen-context -s user python3 <path>` via the CLI. No confirmation of success was captured.
problems: ameen-context MCP server not registered in Claude Code after being built and pushed to GitHub
settings.json rejected mcpServers as an unrecognized field
~/.claude/.mcp.json was created but not picked up by `claude mcp list`
approaches: Added mcpServers key to ~/.claude/settings.json — failed, schema validation error
Created ~/.claude/.mcp.json with server config — failed, not detected
Used `claude mcp add ameen-context -s user python3 <script>` CLI command — in progress, transcript cut off
results: settings.json does not support mcpServers as a top-level field
~/.claude/.mcp.json did not register the server (possibly wrong path or not supported)
Repo confirmed pushed to GitHub: ahassan507/ameen-context-mcp, 11 files
Correct registration method is the CLI: claude mcp add -s user

decisions.decision: Use `claude mcp add <name> -s user <interpreter> <script>` for global MCP registration
MCP server config does not go in settings.json

open_questions: Did `claude mcp add ameen-context -s user python3 ...` succeed after the transcript cut off?
Is the ameen-context server now showing in `claude mcp list`?
Does the server load correctly and respond to tool calls?
